explanation: The requirement describes the sequence and completion condition of the Start-up Mode. During Start-up Mode, the controller must load its default parameters, check communications with sensors, valves, and the GUI, and finally complete initialization. The atomic propositions that directly indicate the completion of each activity (defaultParamsLoaded, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI), as well as the final completion (initDone), can be combined to express Start-up Mode as an 'until' condition: StartUpMode remains active until all these activities are complete (indicated by initDone). Choosing the template that expresses 'StartUpMode holds until initDone' (with the relevant checks implied as being required prior to completion) best captures the natural language requirement. Since there is no explicit atomic proposition for each check being done, we can state that StartUpMode ends (i.e., StartUpMode is no longer true) only after 'initDone' (which assumes all required actions are complete). This is reasonably captured by: '(! (StartUpMode & (X (! StartUpMode)))) U ((StartUpMode & (X (! StartUpMode))) & (X initDone))', i.e., the only way Start-up Mode completes is after all required tasks, as indicated by reaching 'initDone'.

The provided natural language requirement defines that during Start-up Mode, the system initializes itself with default parameters (if any), checks communication with sensors, valves, and the GUI, and completes Start-up Mode only after these activities are done. The atomic propositions relevant to these steps are: 'StartUpMode' (indicates the system is in startup mode), 'defaultParamsLoaded' (default params loaded), 'checkCommsSensors' (communication with sensors checked), 'checkCommsValves' (communication with valves checked), 'checkCommsGUI' (communication with GUI checked), and 'initDone' (configurations loaded and checks terminated = startup can be exited). The key behavioral spec is: Whenever the system is in StartUpMode, eventually all of the required activities (default params loaded, comms checked, and init done) are completed. Thus, I chose a template expressing that globally, whenever StartUpMode holds, these activities should eventually occur. The most direct template expressing this is: G (StartUpMode -> F[0,N_DURATION] (defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone)). Here, N_DURATION can be set, for example, to 10 (representing a reasonable upper bound for completing startup).

The requirement for Start-up Mode is that the controller performs initialization, checking communication and memory, and is done when the required activities are complete. The best mapping is: StartUpMode implies eventually, defaultParamsLoaded, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI, and initDone will all become true (i.e., each required activity is completed at least once during start-up mode). The strong completion suggests that as long as StartUpMode is active, eventually all these checks and initializations must finish. This can be captured by saying: Globally, if StartUpMode is true, then eventually (within finite steps), all of 'defaultParamsLoaded', 'checkCommsSensors', 'checkCommsValves', 'checkCommsGUI', and 'initDone' must be true (conjunction). This aligns with template 14: G (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)).

This formula asserts that for the system to complete start-up mode, it must first load the default parameters and successfully check communication with the sensors, the valves, and the GUI, and that only after all these are completed, the initialization ('initDone') can occur. This reflects the described start-up sequence, where 'initDone' (completing startup) is only true after default configurations and all communications checks are complete. A suitable pattern for this "only after a set of conditions is true, then another condition may be true" is to encode as an implication: If 'initDone' is true, then 'defaultParamsLoaded', 'checkCommsSensors', 'checkCommsValves', and 'checkCommsGUI' must be true (possibly now or previously). Since initialization is completed ('initDone') only after all prerequisites, the property is: globally, if 'initDone' occurs, then all preconditions must hold. N_DURATION is not needed in this context, as we want this to hold always.

The property expresses that, globally, whenever the StartUpMode is active, all the required initialization activities must be completed before finishing the mode. The activities are captured as:
- defaultParamsLoaded (default config params loaded, if any),
- checkCommsSensors (communication with sensors is checked),
- checkCommsValves (communication with valves is checked),
- checkCommsGUI (communication with GUI is checked),
- initDone (internal checks are terminated and configurations have been loaded).
This is represented as: 'if StartUpMode, then eventually each of these must become true.' In LTL, the best fit is to say: whenever StartUpMode, eventually (within a bounded time, say 5 steps), all are done (i.e., AND of activities). Hence, the template G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)) is used with bool_exp1 as StartUpMode and bool_exp3 as the conjunction of the required activities.

The natural language describes the start-up mode in which the controller initializes itself, loads default parameters if any, checks system memory, and specifically includes checking communication with sensors, valves, and GUI. Start-up is completed once these required activities have been performed. The most representative atomic propositions in the given dictionary for these are: 'initStart' (initiating start-up), 'defaultParamsLoaded', 'checkCommsSensors', 'checkCommsValves', 'checkCommsGUI', and 'initDone' (which signals start-up completion after all checks and loads). Since the template must use only the available atomic propositions, we can combine the requirement for start-up mode being active (StartUpMode), the necessary activities (defaultParamsLoaded, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI), and the completion (initDone). The best fit is to require globally (during StartUpMode) that once initDone is true, the activities must have been performed at some point (i.e., they are eventually true prior to or at the same time as initDone). Using a conjunction, we set bool_exp1 as StartUpMode, and bool_exp3 as (defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone). Template 30 expresses: 'Globally, if StartUpMode then ensure all start-up steps are completed.' N_DURATION is not required for this invariance-focused property.

The property expresses that whenever StartUpMode is active, then within 10 steps, all the following must occur: (1) default configuration parameters are loaded (defaultParamsLoaded), (2) the controller checks communication with sensors (checkCommsSensors), (3) checks communication with valves (checkCommsValves), (4) checks communication with the GUI (checkCommsGUI), and (5) completes initialization (initDone). This directly models the natural language specification that Start-up mode is to complete only when these activities successfully finish.

The natural language describes the start-up mode of the controller, where it initializes with default parameters, checks system memory, and verifies communication with sensors, valves, and the GUI. This mode is considered complete once these initialization and checking steps are finished. The most direct mapping using the provided atomic propositions is to ensure that once 'initStart' is true (indicating the initialization process has started), eventually 'initDone' becomes true (indicating that configurations and checks have been successfully completed). This guarantees that the initialization process will always eventually complete if it has started, matching the intent that start-up mode completes after the required activities. The template for 'Globally (if initStart then eventually initDone)' is a standard way to formalize such a requirement.

This property ensures that whenever the system is in Start-up Mode (StartUpMode), it must, within a fixed bounded time (here, 30 cycles), eventually reach a state where the required initialization has been completed (initDone becomes true). This captures the requirement that start-up mode completes only once all the necessary setup (loading default parameters, checking communications with sensors, valves, and GUI) is finished, as described in the natural language specification. The template makes use of the atomic propositions StartUpMode (indicating the system is in start-up mode) and initDone (indicating all initialization steps are done) linked by a temporal implication and bounded eventually operator.

The property requires that, while the system is in StartUpMode, the key activities of the start-up process—default parameter loading, successful communication checking with valves, sensors, and GUI, and completion of internal checks (initDone)—must eventually occur. This is captured by requiring that, globally whenever StartUpMode is active, it is always the case (in a bounded duration) that all these sub-tasks are performed, or globally completed, within a specific duration (chosen as 10 steps for this purpose). This captures the input text's notion that start-up mode is not done until these checks and initializations are finished.
